fault_hypothesis: The conditional on line 76 is flawed, causing the pop() on line 77 to incorrectly remove a valid element when both input lists share the same final value.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left__1 and right__1 and merged__1[-1] == left__1[-1] == right__1[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition for removing an element should not be met for cases that are actually valid merges.
After the merge loop and extends, the merged list must contain all elements from both inputs.

spec.expr: sum(1 for x in merged__1 if x == left__1[-1]) == sum(1 for x in left__1 if x == left__1[-1]) + sum(1 for x in right__1 if x == left__1[-1])

constraints.intent: The final count of a shared last element must be the sum of its counts in the inputs.
The final merged list must contain exactly the sum of the number of elements from the two input lists.
